financial_metrics.label: Strong Beat

revenue.label: Strong Growth
revenue.analysis: Network gross revenue stood at INR 2,692 crore, reflecting an increase of 21% year-on-year and 5% versus the trailing quarter [p.3]. New Units contributed INR 144 crore, while Existing Units delivered a 14% like-for-like revenue growth [p.3]. Digital revenue from online marketing activities rose to INR 803 crore, accounting for approximately 30% of the overall revenue [p.4]. International patient revenue reached INR 231 crore, registering a growth of 25% year-on-year and 11% quarter-on-quarter [p.4].

ebitda.label: Strong Growth
ebitda.analysis: Network operating EBITDA stood at INR 694 crore, reflecting a growth of 23% year-on-year and 13% quarter-on-quarter [p.4]. The EBITDA margin was 26.9% for the quarter, with Existing Units reporting an EBITDA margin of 27.5%, higher by 90 basis points year-on-year and 200 basis points sequentially [p.4].

profitability.label: One-time Boost
profitability.analysis: Profit after tax (PAT) for the Network was INR 554 crore, against INR 349 crore in Q2 last year and INR 345 crore in the previous quarter [p.4]. This includes a one-time favourable tax impact of INR 149 crore due to the merger of Crosslay Remedies Ltd. and Jaypee Healthcare Ltd. Excluding this impact, PAT stood at INR 406 crore, reflecting 16% year-on-year growth [p.4].

cash_flow.label: Moderate
cash_flow.analysis: The Network generated free cash flows of INR 291 crore during the quarter [p.4]. There was a buildup of accounts receivable in the institutional segment, which impacted the cash flow from operations [p.4].

capex.label: High Investment
capex.analysis: INR 456 crore was deployed towards ongoing capacity expansion projects and facility upgrades at new units during the quarter [p.4]. For the first half, INR 891 crore was deployed towards ongoing expansion projects and facility upgrades, and INR 131 crore was spent towards land purchase for brownfield expansion at Max Vaishali [p.6].

debt.label: Increased Leverage
debt.analysis: Net Debt for the Network stood at INR 2,067 crore compared to INR 1,755 crore at the end of June 2025 [p.4].

working_capital.label: Receivables Buildup
working_capital.analysis: There was a buildup of accounts receivable in the institutional segment, which impacted the cash flow from operations [p.4].

operational_kpis.label: Strong Utilization
operational_kpis.analysis: Average occupancy for the Network stood at 77% compared to 79% in Q2 last year and 76% in the trailing quarter [p.3]. Occupied bed days (OBDs) were up by 19% year-on-year and 4% quarter-on-quarter [p.3]. Average Revenue Per Occupied Bed (ARPOB) for the quarter was INR 77,300, registering 1% growth year-on-year [p.3].

guidance.label: Not Available
guidance.analysis: No specific data disclosed in this document.

financial_metrics.qualitative_analysis: The financial results for Q2 FY26 were strong, with revenue and EBITDA both showing significant year-on-year growth. The performance was driven by both Existing and New Units, with digital and international patient revenues contributing materially. However, the PAT was boosted by a one-time tax impact, which should be considered when evaluating profitability. The buildup of receivables in the institutional segment is a point of concern for cash flow management.
financial_metrics.sentiment_narrative: Management expressed confidence in the company's growth trajectory, highlighting a 'strong growth momentum' through the first half of FY26 [p.2]. They noted the 'continued strength of core operations' and emphasized the strategic importance of brownfield expansions [p.2]. The resolution of the cashless facilities impasse was also mentioned as a positive development [p.3]. Despite the challenges, the management remains optimistic, expecting a 'favourable impact of over INR 200 crore' from the CGHS price revision once fully implemented [p.3]. Overall, the tone was positive, with a focus on strategic growth and operational efficiency.
financial_metrics.title: Financial Metrics

risk_intelligence.label: Well Managed

risks.label: High Risk
risks.analysis: There was a temporary stoppage of cashless facilities due to an impasse with certain insurance players, which has since been resolved [p.18]. However, such disruptions can affect patient inflow and revenue stability. Management has not detailed specific measures to prevent future occurrences.

risks.label: Moderate Risk
risks.analysis: The CGHS pricing revision is expected to have a favorable impact of over INR 200 crore once fully implemented [p.21]. However, the timeline for full implementation remains uncertain, which could delay the anticipated revenue benefits.

risks.label: Moderate Risk
risks.analysis: The company's net debt increased to INR 2,067 crore from INR 1,755 crore at the end of June 2025, primarily due to ongoing capacity expansion projects and facility upgrades [p.4]. Management has not indicated any immediate plans to reduce this debt, which could impact financial flexibility if not managed carefully.

risks.label: Moderate Risk
risks.analysis: The company is expanding its capacity with new hospital beds, which are expected to improve profitability [p.22]. However, the addition of new hospitals at lower margins could initially dilute overall margins, posing a risk to short-term profitability.

risks_downplayed: Cash flow impact from accounts receivable
undisclosed_risks: Potential delays in CGHS revenue realization
Initial margin dilution from new hospital expansions

risk_overview.analysis: Overall, the risk communication was moderately transparent. Management highlighted key risks such as insurance negotiations and CGHS pricing revisions but did not provide detailed mitigation strategies for potential future disruptions. The increase in net debt and its implications on financial flexibility were not adequately addressed, indicating a need for more comprehensive risk disclosure.

risk_intelligence.sentiment_narrative: Management communicated a cautiously optimistic outlook, emphasizing strong revenue growth and successful resolution of insurance issues. They highlighted ongoing capacity expansions and expected benefits from CGHS pricing revisions. However, there was a lack of detailed discussion on mitigating financial risks associated with increased debt and potential delays in realizing CGHS benefits, suggesting a focus on positive developments while downplaying certain operational challenges.
risk_intelligence.title: Risk Intelligence

esg.label: Good

environmental.label: N/A
environmental.analysis: No ESG disclosure found for this pillar in this earnings call.

social.label: Good
social.analysis: Max Healthcare Institute has demonstrated a commitment to social responsibility by providing free treatment to approximately 44,000 patients from economically weaker sections, valued at INR 61 crore at hospital tariff [p.4]. This initiative reflects a significant effort to support local communities and improve healthcare access for underprivileged populations. The disclosure provides a clear metric of the number of beneficiaries and the financial value of the support, indicating a well-defined social impact. However, the call lacks specific future targets or timelines for expanding these efforts, which limits the depth of the social disclosure.

governance.label: Good
governance.analysis: The company has streamlined its corporate structure by completing the divestment of hospitals in Chitta and Anoopshahr and merging two wholly owned subsidiaries, resulting in a one-time benefit of INR 149 crore [p.3]. This indicates a proactive approach to governance and financial management. Additionally, the company reported a 21% year-on-year revenue growth and a 23% increase in operating EBITDA, showcasing strong financial governance and operational efficiency [p.2]. The governance disclosure is clear and includes specific financial metrics, but it could be enhanced by providing more details on board composition or executive compensation.

esg.sentiment_narrative: Max Healthcare Institute's earnings call reflects a strong commitment to social responsibility, particularly in supporting economically weaker sections through free healthcare services. The governance practices are robust, with clear financial metrics and strategic corporate restructuring efforts. However, the absence of environmental disclosures and specific future targets for social initiatives suggests room for improvement in comprehensive ESG reporting. The call lacks mention of any third-party verification or adherence to established ESG reporting frameworks, which could enhance credibility and transparency. Overall, the communication is positive but could benefit from more detailed and structured ESG commitments.
esg.title: ESG Analysis

speaker_sentiment.label: Very Bullish

speakers.name: Abhay Soi
speakers.analysis: Abhay Soi opened the call with a confident tone, highlighting a 21% year-on-year revenue growth and a 23% increase in operating EBITDA for Q2 FY'26, marking 20 consecutive quarters of growth [p.2]. He emphasized the strategic expansion through brownfield projects and the resolution of insurance issues, which had temporarily affected cashless facilities [p.2, p.4]. Soi's confidence was further bolstered by the expected INR 200 crore positive impact from CGHS price revisions [p.4]. Despite some analyst questions on insurance and occupancy rates, Soi maintained a positive outlook, attributing any temporary setbacks to strategic growth initiatives [p.20].
speakers.label: Very Bullish

speakers.name: Yogesh Sareen
speakers.analysis: Yogesh Sareen provided detailed financial metrics, noting that Max Lab and Max@Home reported EBITDA margins of 16% and 20%, respectively [p.13]. His tone was neutral, focusing on factual reporting of financial data without embellishment. Sareen's responses were straightforward, addressing questions about profitability and capital efficiency in the company's asset-light divisions [p.13]. There was no significant pressure from analysts on his statements, as they were primarily data-driven.
speakers.label: Neutral

speakers.name: Kritika Damani
speakers.analysis: Kritika Damani asked about the impact of high-acuity care on occupancy and patient throughput, as well as the revenue contribution from international patients [p.12, p.13]. Her questions were probing, seeking clarity on how the company balances increased length of stay with maintaining throughput. Damani also inquired about the profitability of asset-light divisions, indicating a focus on understanding the company's operational efficiency and growth strategy [p.13].
speakers.label: Neutral

speaker_sentiment.management_tone_summary: Overall, the management communicated with confidence, emphasizing strong financial performance and strategic growth initiatives. They addressed potential concerns with factual data and maintained a positive outlook on future prospects.
notable_exchanges: Abhay Soi addressed concerns about insurance issues affecting growth, clarifying that the impact was mitigated by a shift to self-pay patients [p.20].
Kritika Damani's questions on international patient revenue and asset-light division profitability prompted detailed responses from management, highlighting strategic growth areas [p.12, p.13].
speaker_sentiment.overall_sentiment_shift: The sentiment remained positive throughout the call, with management effectively addressing analyst concerns and reinforcing confidence in the company's growth trajectory.
speaker_sentiment.sentiment_narrative: The dominant tone of the management was confident, driven by strong financial results and strategic expansion plans. Abhay Soi highlighted consistent revenue and EBITDA growth, underscoring the company's robust operational performance. Despite some challenges, such as temporary insurance issues, the management maintained a positive outlook, supported by expected favorable impacts from CGHS price revisions. The focus on brownfield expansions and asset-light divisions further reinforced the company's growth strategy. Overall, the management's communication was effective in conveying a sense of stability and forward momentum.
speaker_sentiment.title: Speaker Sentiment

qa_transparency.label: Very Transparent

qa_pairs.label: Direct Response
qa_pairs.analysis: Andrey Purushottam asked about the ARPOB growth rate and whether the company's internal discussions align with the perception of slowing growth [p.9]. Yogesh Sareen responded with specific figures, stating that ARPOB growth is 3% year-on-year, but when excluding new hospitals, it is 7% [p.9]. The response was transparent, providing clear numerical data to support the explanation.

qa_pairs.analyst: Amit Thawani, Clear Blue Capital Advisors LLP
qa_pairs.label: Direct Response
qa_pairs.analysis: Amit Thawani inquired about the ARPOB growth for existing units in the current quarter [p.12]. Yogesh Sareen clarified that the ARPOB increase for existing units was 6.8% in the September quarter [p.12]. The response was transparent, with specific numbers provided to address the question.

qa_pairs.label: Direct Response
qa_pairs.analysis: Neha Manpuria asked about the network capex intensity for the first half and expectations for the second half [p.20]. Yogesh Sareen provided specific figures, stating that INR 900 crore was spent in the first half and the second half would likely see a higher expenditure of around INR 1,100 crore [p.20]. The response was transparent and backed by precise financial data.

qa_pairs.label: Partial Response
qa_pairs.analysis: Kunal Dhamesha asked about the operationalized bed numbers for Q3 and Q4 [p.19]. Yogesh Sareen mentioned the difficulty in providing exact numbers due to pending approvals and NOCs, while Abhay Soi avoided making forward-looking statements [p.19]. The response was partially transparent, acknowledging uncertainties but lacking specific projections.

qa_pairs.analyst: Damayanti Kerai, HSBC and Capital Markets (India) Private Limited
qa_pairs.label: Direct Response
qa_pairs.analysis: Damayanti Kerai asked about the impact of senior doctor departures in NCR hospitals [p.6]. Abhay Soi responded that such turnover is normal and that new teams are hired almost immediately, with no expected impact [p.6]. The response was direct and addressed the question clearly.

qa_transparency.qualitative_assessment: Overall, management provided clear and data-backed responses to most questions, particularly those related to financial performance and operational metrics. However, there was a tendency to avoid providing specific forward-looking statements, especially regarding future operational metrics. This reflects a cautious approach to guidance, balancing transparency with prudence.
qa_transparency.sentiment_narrative: Management's communication style in the Q&A was generally informative and supportive, with a focus on providing specific data where possible. They were open about current performance metrics and operational strategies, but cautious about future projections. This approach suggests a commitment to transparency while managing expectations. The use of specific figures in responses helped build credibility, although some areas, particularly forward-looking guidance, remained vague.
qa_transparency.title: Q&A Transparency

overall_sentiment.label: Very Bullish

tone_overview.analysis: Management expressed high confidence, highlighted by 21% year-on-year revenue growth and a 23% increase in operating EBITDA, marking 20 consecutive quarters of growth [p.2]. The expansion efforts, including new bed capacity at Max Mohali and upcoming projects at Nanavati-Max and Max Smart, were emphasized as key drivers of future growth [p.2]. Despite some challenges in the insurance segment, the resolution of cashless facilities and a favorable CGHS price revision are expected to positively impact future revenues by over INR 200 crore [p.4]. The digital revenue growth of 53% year-on-year and international patient revenue increase of 25% further underscore the robust performance [p.4]. Overall, the tone was bullish with raised guidance and no significant headwinds mentioned.

key_surprises: Resolution of cashless facilities impasse in the insurance segment.
overall_sentiment.qualitative_synthesis: The earnings call for Max Healthcare Institute was notably positive, with management demonstrating high confidence in their growth trajectory. The company reported strong financial performance, with significant revenue and EBITDA growth, and highlighted successful expansion projects. While there were some temporary challenges in the insurance segment, these were resolved, and future revenue is expected to benefit from favorable policy changes. The overall market signal is bullish, driven by robust operational performance and strategic expansions.
overall_sentiment.sentiment_narrative: Management was pleased to report a continuation of strong growth momentum, with a 21% increase in revenue and a 23% rise in operating EBITDA for the second quarter [p.2]. The expansion of capacity at key locations like Max Mohali and the upcoming projects at Nanavati-Max and Max Smart were highlighted as significant growth drivers [p.2]. Despite a temporary impasse in the insurance segment, the resolution of cashless facilities and a favorable CGHS price revision are expected to contribute positively to future revenues [p.4]. Digital revenue saw a substantial increase, accounting for 30% of overall revenue, and international patient revenue grew by 25% year-on-year [p.4]. The management's tone was optimistic, with a focus on continued growth and expansion, signaling a bullish outlook for investors.
overall_sentiment.title: Overall Sentiment

investment_synthesis.label: Buy

thesis.scope: Based on Q2 and H1 FY26 results
thesis.analysis: Max Healthcare Institute has demonstrated consistent growth with a 21% year-on-year increase in revenue and a 23% rise in operating EBITDA for Q2 FY26, marking 20 consecutive quarters of growth [p.2]. The company's strategic focus on brownfield expansions and streamlining operations, including the divestment of non-core assets, has strengthened its core operations [p.3]. However, challenges such as slower ARPOB growth due to new hospital mix and increased finance costs from recent acquisitions may temper short-term profitability [p.9]. Overall, the company's robust expansion strategy and operational efficiencies position it well for future growth.

bull_case.analysis: Max Healthcare's revenue and EBITDA growth of 21% and 23% respectively in Q2 FY26 highlight strong operational performance [p.2]. The company's brownfield expansions, such as the new towers at Max Mohali and Nanavati-Max, are expected to enhance margins due to higher profitability compared to greenfield projects [p.22]. Additionally, the recent CGHS price revision is anticipated to contribute over INR 200 crore in revenue, providing a significant boost to financials [p.3]. The international patient segment also showed robust growth, with a 25% increase year-on-year [p.2].

bear_case.analysis: The growth in Average Revenue Per Occupied Bed (ARPOB) is slowing, with a reported 3% year-on-year increase for existing hospitals, which could impact revenue growth if not addressed [p.9]. The company's profit growth of 16% lags behind EBITDA and revenue growth due to increased finance and depreciation costs from recent acquisitions [p.9]. Additionally, the temporary suspension of cashless facilities with certain insurers highlights potential operational risks in managing insurance partnerships [p.7]. The ongoing expansion projects require significant capital expenditure, which could strain cash flows if not managed carefully [p.20].

catalysts.near_term: The commissioning of new brownfield towers at Nanavati-Max and Max Smart within the next 30 days is expected to enhance capacity and improve margins [p.3]. The full implementation of the CGHS price revision could significantly boost revenue in the coming quarters [p.3].
catalysts.medium_term: The expansion of Max Lucknow to 550 beds and the launch of the onco radiation program will drive growth over the next 6-18 months [p.3]. Continued growth in international patient revenue and digital services could further enhance profitability and market reach [p.2].

key_watchpoints: Cash flow management and capex execution
investment_synthesis.sentiment_narrative: Management expressed confidence in the company's growth trajectory, highlighting 20 consecutive quarters of consistent growth [p.2]. They emphasized the strategic importance of brownfield expansions for margin improvement and operational efficiency [p.22]. The resolution of insurance-related issues and the anticipated benefits from the CGHS price revision were noted as positive developments [p.3]. However, they acknowledged the challenges posed by slower ARPOB growth and increased finance costs due to recent acquisitions [p.9]. Overall, the tone was optimistic about future prospects, supported by ongoing expansion efforts and operational enhancements.
investment_synthesis.title: Investment Synthesis
